title: Bump github.com/gogo/protobuf/proto to v1.3.2
body:
Bumping github.com/gogo/protobuf/proto to v1.3.2 to enusure
a low likelyhood CVE is reached.  Partially addresses:

https://bugzilla.redhat.com/show_bug.cgi?id=1924544

Signed-off-by: TomSweeneyRedHat <tsweeney@redhat.com>

comments.body: Will need to do this in the release-1.2 branch too, maybe others?

Very quickly looking at the bug, are we really using the code? AFAICS it’s only imported for some Swarm types. Sure, it’s better to upgrade and not have to worry about it any more, at least on the main branch; on the other branches it might or might not be worth the effort, I don’t have a strong opinion.

@mtrmac I didn't see that we were using the code much, if at all, I found it vendored only in the swarm as you noted:

```
./vendor/github.com/Microsoft/hcsshim/Protobuild.toml:  "google/protobuf/descriptor.proto" = "github.com/gogo/protobuf/protoc-gen-gogo/descriptor"
./vendor/github.com/containerd/cgroups/stats/v1/metrics.pb.go:	proto "github.com/gogo/protobuf/proto"
./vendor/github.com/docker/docker/api/types/swarm/runtime/plugin.pb.go:import proto "github.com/gogo/protobuf/proto"
```
My thinking was to just vendor here in upstream and in the release-1.2 branch just to be ultra-safe.
